lessons.title: Day 1: Pitch Center
lessons.body: Pitch is a target, not a vibe. Your job is to land the note quickly and then *stay in the middle* without pushing volume.
keyPoints: Aim for the center of the pitch, not above it.
A softer start is usually more accurate than a loud start.
doThis: Take a quiet breath (no shoulder lift).
Start the note at 70% volume.
If you’re sharp, think ‘lower + lighter’—not ‘force it down’.
avoidThis: Changing the vowel mid-note (it pulls pitch).
coachScript: Imagine the note for half a second, then sing it softly.
If you miss, don’t panic—adjust gently.

lessons.title: Day 2: Fast Lock
lessons.body: Most ‘bad pitch’ is just *late pitch*. The first 300–500ms matters. Train your brain to hear the note before you sing.
keyPoints: Hear it → sing it. Not the other way around.
Small correction is fine. Big swoops cost points.
doThis: Before you start, whisper the note in your head.
Start with a clean onset (no breathy slide).
If you’re off, correct once—then hold.
avoidThis: Sliding up to the target every time.
coachScript: Hear the note. Then sing it immediately—soft and clean.

lessons.title: Day 3: Hold Steady
lessons.body: Stability is calm airflow + a stable vowel. If the vowel changes, the pitch moves.
keyPoints: Steady pitch comes from steady breath.
Keep the vowel consistent (same mouth shape).
doThis: Pick one vowel (like ‘oo’ or ‘ee’) and keep it.
Imagine the note is a laser line—straight.
Let the breath be even from start to finish.
avoidThis: Clenching the jaw to ‘hold’ the note.
coachScript: Hold the middle of the note. Calm.

lessons.title: Day 4: Clean Slides
lessons.body: A slide should feel like one continuous line—not steps. Smoothness beats speed.
keyPoints: Keep volume steady through the slide.
doThis: Start the slide gently.
Keep your throat relaxed—let pitch change, not tension.
Arrive at the target and *stop moving*.
avoidThis: Adding vibrato during the slide (hard to control).
coachScript: Slide like a single smooth line.

lessons.title: Day 5: Intervals
lessons.body: Intervals are jumps. The cleanest jump happens when you ‘hear the landing’ first.
keyPoints: If you miss, reset your ear and try again.
doThis: Sing the first note quietly.
Think the second note.
Jump with the same vowel.
avoidThis: Turning every jump into a slide.

lessons.title: Day 6: Melody Shape
lessons.body: Shape first, cents second. If you get the contour right, accuracy becomes easy.
keyPoints: Up/down is more important than perfect cents at first.
Short notes still need clear targets.
doThis: Hum the phrase once (easy tone).
Then sing it with one consistent vowel.
Tighten accuracy on your second attempt.

lessons.title: Day 7: Week 1 Check
lessons.body: This is not a score chase. It’s a control check. One clean rep is a win.
keyPoints: If you miss, reduce volume and try again.
doThis: Pick the easiest vowel for you.
Focus on early lock.
Stop after one clean rep.
coachScript: If it’s messy, go softer and retry.

lessons.title: Day 8: Breath Phrasing
lessons.body: Long notes don’t need more air—just steadier air. Think ‘slow leak’, not ‘dump’.
keyPoints: Start gently; don’t blast air at the onset.
doThis: Start at 60–70% volume.
Keep ribs open while you sustain.
If the pitch wobbles, reduce volume slightly.
coachScript: Keep the ribs open while you hold.

lessons.title: Day 9: Stability Under Motion
lessons.body: After movement (a slide), your job is to re-center instantly. Think ‘snap to calm’.
doThis: Slide smoothly.
The moment you arrive, lock.
Hold the target with calm airflow.
avoidThis: Keeping the slide moving after you arrive.
coachScript: Hold the center of the pitch.

lessons.title: Day 10: Agility
lessons.body: Agility is accuracy at speed. The secret is *clean landings* on every note.
keyPoints: Short notes still need a target.
doThis: Start slower than you want.
Hit each note cleanly.
Only speed up when accuracy stays.

lessons.title: Day 11: Resonance Awareness
lessons.body: Resonance is ‘easy loud’. If it feels squeezed, you’re using force instead of space.
keyPoints: Volume is optional. Clarity is not.
doThis: Try a gentle ‘ng’ feeling (like ‘sing’).
Keep cheeks and jaw relaxed.
If it tightens, lower the volume and retry.

lessons.title: Day 12: Vibrato Control
lessons.body: Vibrato is a choice. Train straight tone first, then add gentle vibrato intentionally.
keyPoints: If pitch wobbles, reduce volume + stabilize breath.
doThis: Hold straight tone for 2–3 seconds.
Then add tiny oscillation.
Return to straight tone on command.
avoidThis: Forcing vibrato by shaking the jaw.
coachScript: Back to straight tone. Your choice.

lessons.title: Day 13: Hear First
lessons.body: If you can hear it clearly, you can sing it cleanly. This day connects jumps and melody.
keyPoints: Hear the next note before you move.
doThis: Pause between notes for a split second.
Lock each landing.
Keep your vowel consistent across the phrase.
avoidThis: Sliding when the task is a jump.
coachScript: Lock each landing before the next note.

lessons.title: Day 14: Week 2 Check
lessons.body: This is your ‘clean + musical’ check. Stability plus a phrase that feels like music.
doThis: Take one clean breath.
Stay calm and steady.
Stop after one clean run.
avoidThis: Doing too many reps when you’re tired.
coachScript: One clean run and you’re done.
